meta.note: Acceptance criteria are structured data compared deterministically by the commissioning engine. Standard references are representative and are not verbatim clause citations. Pass/fail is derived from submitted readings; no results are pre-recorded in this file.

procedures.title: UPS-02A Concurrent Maintainability & Retransfer Test
procedures.objective: Demonstrate that UPS-02A supports the critical load without interruption through mains-failure, static-bypass and maintenance-bypass transitions, and that transfer/retransfer timing meets the specified limits for a concurrently maintainable topology.
prerequisites: UPS-02A installation and battery system complete (ACT-033, ACT-034).
Calibrated power quality analyser and transient recorder connected at output.
Load bank connected to the critical bus at the specified test load.

steps.instruction: Establish 100% rated load on the UPS output via load bank; confirm steady-state online (double-conversion) operation.
Interrupt the rectifier input to simulate mains failure; confirm no interruption to the critical bus as the UPS transfers to stored energy.
Measure inverter-to-static-bypass transfer time on a forced synchronised transfer.
Restore inverter and measure the retransfer time from static bypass back to inverter (return transfer). This is the concurrent-maintainability retransfer hold point.
Verify output voltage regulation during and after transfers.
Perform maintenance-bypass transfer and return; confirm the load is not interrupted at any point (concurrent maintainability).
Confirm battery autonomy at rated load meets the specified minimum backup time on discharge.

procedures.title: Standby Generator Load-Bank Test (Level 3)
procedures.objective: Demonstrate generator start time, load acceptance and sustained full-load operation.
prerequisites: Generator installation and fuel system complete (ACT-032).
Fuel level confirmed for test duration.

steps.instruction: Issue start signal and measure time to reach rated voltage and frequency, ready to accept load.
Apply block load and verify frequency dip and recovery within class limits.
Run at 100% rated standby load and confirm stable operation.
Confirm steady-state voltage regulation at full load.

procedures.title: Integrated Systems Test - Level 4 (Failure Mode / Pull-the-Plug)
procedures.objective: Demonstrate integrated response to utility failure: generator start, UPS ride-through, cooling continuity, and no interruption to the critical load.
prerequisites: UPS, generator and cooling commissioning (Level 3) complete.
Full design load (or representative load bank) on critical and mechanical systems.

steps.instruction: Open the utility incomer to simulate total mains loss; confirm the critical load is not interrupted (UPS ride-through).
Confirm generator starts and assumes load within the required time.
Confirm data-hall temperature remains within the environmental envelope throughout the transition.
Restore utility and confirm controlled return of sources with no load interruption.

procedures.title: Cooling Plant Concurrent Maintainability Test
procedures.objective: Demonstrate that any single chiller/pump/section can be isolated for maintenance without reducing delivered cooling below the critical load.
prerequisites: Cooling installation complete (ACT-040, ACT-041) and BMS commissioned (ACT-042).
Design heat load or load banks applied in the data hall.

steps.instruction: Isolate one duty chiller; confirm the standby starts and delivered capacity remains at/above the critical load.
Confirm chilled-water supply temperature is maintained during the transition.
Isolate one primary pump; confirm hydraulic continuity via the redundant path.
Confirm data-hall temperature remains within the environmental envelope throughout.

procedures.title: Reliability / 96-Hour Demonstration Test
procedures.objective: Demonstrate sustained stable operation of the integrated facility at design load over a continuous 96-hour period.
prerequisites: All Level 3 and Level 4 tests passed.
Design load (or representative load banks) applied continuously.
Trending enabled on all critical BMS/EPMS points.

steps.instruction: Operate the facility at design load for the full duration without unplanned trip or interruption; record total continuous run time.
Confirm number of unplanned critical-load interruptions over the period.
Confirm the data-hall environmental envelope was maintained throughout the demonstration.
